name: Fall Vegetable Risotto (Weir)
recipeIngredient: white parts and one inch green ends
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the butternut squash in half lengthwise, scoop out and discard seeds.
2. In a medium-size saucepan over high heat, bring broth and 3 cups water to a simmer. Add squash and cook until almost tender, about 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from heat, season with salt and pepper, and set aside. Reserve broth separately.
3. In a large frying pan, place leeks and cover with 1/2 cup water. Simmer covered until leeks are soft, about 12 minutes, adding more water if needed.
4. Reserve the leeks and cooking liquid, then add it to the reserved broth.
5. Warm the broth over low heat on a back burner. Warm olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add rice and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 to 3 minutes.
6. With a ladle, add about 3/4 cup broth and stir to release the rice from the bottom and sides of the pan.
7. When the rice has absorbed the first addition of broth, add another ladleful of stock. Stir often to prevent sticking, adding more stock a ladleful at a time until the grains are tender but still firm, about 18 to 22 minutes. If you run out of broth, add hot water.
8. When the rice is just tender, add an additional ladle of broth or water and the reserved squash. Remove from heat, cover, and let stand for 5 minutes.
9. Season with salt and pepper. To serve, place the risotto in a bowl and garnish with toasted pecans and Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese. Serves 6.
